HEADSET_ISSUE: Check physical connection of headset to device
Verify headset cable is not damaged
Try plugging into a different USB/audio port
Check Device Manager for driver errors
Go to Settings → Sound → Volume and verify device is set as default
Disable then re-enable the device in Sound settings
Test headset on another computer to isolate the issue
Check if headset has a separate power source and ensure it's on
Uninstall and reinstall the audio device driver
DISPLAY_ISSUE: Check if monitor is powered on and cables are secure
Try pressing Windows key + P to switch display modes
Check display cable connections (HDMI, DisplayPort, VGA)
Verify monitor input source is set correctly
Test with a different cable or port
Check Device Manager for display adapter errors
Try connecting to a different monitor
KEYBOARD_MOUSE_ISSUE: Check physical connection of keyboard/mouse to computer
Try plugging into a different USB port
Check if wireless receiver is connected
Check Device Manager for driver errors
Try a different keyboard/mouse to isolate the issue
Check if Num Lock or Caps Lock is causing issues
Check for hardware damage to cables
NETWORK_ISSUE: Check the network (Wi-Fi/Ethernet) icon in the taskbar to confirm if you are connected to the internet
Ensure the Ethernet cable is properly connected; unplug and replug it securely under your desk
Restart your computer from the Start Menu to refresh the network connection
Perform a basic network reset by turning off your system, waiting 10–15 seconds, and turning it back on
If you experience call delays, voice lag, or distortion, it may be due to unstable internet connection
Press Windows Key + R, type 'cmd', and press Enter to open Command Prompt
Run 'ipconfig' and check for the IPv4 Address (e.g., 192.168.x.x)
Copy your IPv4 address and share it with IT support if needed
Contact IT support if the issue persists after completing all steps
TEAMS_ISSUE: Close Microsoft Teams completely (right-click the Teams icon in the taskbar → Quit) and reopen it
Go to Settings → Apps → Installed Apps → Microsoft Teams → Advanced Options → click 'Repair'
If the issue persists, click 'Reset' under Advanced Options and reopen Teams
Uninstall Microsoft Teams, restart your system, then reinstall and sign in again
Ask external users to join using the same email and sign in or create a Teams account if needed
Sign out of Teams and sign back in with the correct account
Contact IT support if the issue persists after completing all steps
OUTLOOK_ISSUE: Check email account settings are configured correctly
Update Outlook to the latest version
Check mailbox quota hasn't been exceeded
Try removing and re-adding the email account
Clear Outlook cache by disabling offline mode
Check if two-factor authentication is blocking access
Review mailbox rules that might be filtering emails
SOFTWARE_INSTALLATION: Check system requirements match your device
Ensure you have admin rights to install software
Download the latest installer from official source
Run installer as Administrator (right-click → Run as admin)
Check available disk space (minimum 1 GB recommended)
Disable VPN or proxy that might block downloads
Contact software vendor support if installation fails
UNKNOWN: If this requires admin/security access (for example releasing a blocked/quarantined email, unblocking a sender, resetting MFA/passwords, or granting access), contact IT Support
Provide the affected account (email/username) and device type (Windows/Mac/mobile)
Include exact error messages and screenshots if possible
Include the time the issue happened and whether it affects others
Contact IT Support with these details for further assistance
